skill.name: lab-budget-forecaster
skill.description: Calculates monthly burn rates and predicts grant fund depletion dates from expense records and budget data.
skill.summary: Forecast when lab grant funds will run out based on current spending patterns.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 43 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. System reconnaissance flags were false alarms on audit result metadata. Weak cryptographic algorithm flags matched no actual crypto code. Ruby backtick execution flags were markdown formatting syntax, not command execution. Path traversal flags were security checklist documentation, not actual traversal. No malicious patterns, data exfiltration, or code injection risks were found. The skill is a legitimate budget forecasting tool that reads CSV expense files and performs local calculations.

content.user_title: Forecast Lab Budgets and Predict Grant Fund Depletion
content.value_statement: Managing lab grant budgets is complex, with multiple expense categories and uncertainty about fund depletion dates. This skill forecasts burn rates and predicts when funds will run out, helping you plan cost-cutting measures and bridge funding requests.
actual_capabilities: Calculate monthly burn rate from expense records
Predict fund depletion date based on current spending
Parse expense data from CSV files
Run cost-cutting scenarios to extend grant runway
Provide bridge funding and no-cost extension alerts
limitations: Requires accurate expense data to produce reliable forecasts
Does not account for variable interest or investment income
Assumes linear spending patterns for depletion predictions
Cannot import data from institutional financial systems

use_cases.title: Grant Fund Tracking
use_cases.description: Track remaining grant funds and predict when money will run out to request additional funding in time.

use_cases.title: Lab Resource Planning
use_cases.description: Plan equipment purchases and hiring decisions based on available budget runway.

use_cases.title: No-Cost Extension Planning
use_cases.description: Forecast no-cost extension needs and generate budget reports for quarterly reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Budget Health Check
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a grant with a total budget of $150,000 running from 2024-01-01 to 2025-12-31. My current monthly expenses are $12,000 for personnel and $3,000 for supplies. How many months of runway do I have?
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick budget health check for a single grant

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Expense Forecast from CSV
prompt_templates.prompt: Using this CSV file of my lab expenses, forecast my budget depletion. My total grant budget is $500,000, grant start date is 2024-01-01, and end date is 2026-12-31. Include the monthly burn rate and the predicted depletion date.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed forecast using uploaded expense data

prompt_templates.title: Cost-Cutting Scenario Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: My lab budget is $200,000 with $15,000 monthly burn rate. I need to extend runway by 6 months. What monthly spending target do I need to hit and what categories should I reduce first?
prompt_templates.scenario: Runway extension planning through cost reduction

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Grant Portfolio Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: I have three grants with budgets $300,000, $150,000, and $75,000. All started on 2024-01-01 and end on 2026-12-31. My monthly expenses across all grants total $25,000. When will each grant be depleted and what bridge funding amounts are needed for the shortfall period?
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-grant portfolio management with funding gap analysis

output_examples.input: What is my budget status for a $250,000 grant that started 6 months ago with $80,000 spent so far?
output_examples.output: A budget forecast showing total budget $250,000, total spent $80,000, remaining $170,000 at 68 percent, monthly burn rate $13,333, and predicted depletion date 12.75 months from now.

output_examples.input: I need to plan a $35,000 equipment purchase. My remaining grant balance is $120,000 and monthly burn is $10,000.
output_examples.output: An analysis showing the equipment purchase would reduce runway from 12 months to 8.5 months, with a recommendation to delay the purchase or find additional funding sources.

best_practices: Keep expense records up to date before running forecasts for accurate results.
Review documented assumptions in each report before making financial decisions.
Run multiple scenarios with different burn rates to prepare for changes in spending.
anti_patterns: Do not use the forecast without verifying that expense data is complete and accurate.
Do not assume the linear burn rate model applies to grants with irregular seasonal spending.
Do not rely on a single forecast output alone for high-stakes funding decisions.

faq.question: What data does this skill need?
faq.answer: Total budget amount, grant start and end dates, and expense records with amounts and dates.

faq.question: How does it calculate burn rate?
faq.answer: Burn rate is computed by dividing total expenses by the number of days elapsed and multiplying by 30.

faq.question: Can I use it for multiple grants?
faq.answer: Yes. Run the skill separately for each grant to get individual forecasts for each one.

faq.question: Does this skill need internet access?
faq.answer: No. All calculations run locally on your expense data without any external calls.

faq.question: How accurate are the predictions?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on complete and accurate expense data. The model assumes a linear spending pattern.

faq.question: What file formats does it support?
faq.answer: Expense data can be loaded from CSV files or entered directly through prompts.
